compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The compact/delete/init contended-lock tests plant a lock then start a
`threading.Timer(0.4, unlink)` and assert only `rc == 0` plus snapshot/tombstone presence
(`tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_index_lock.py:71-85`,
`tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_delete_init_index_lock.py:83-119`). On a slow runner the
0.4s timer removes the lock before git's first attempt, so the test passes through the
ordinary success path without ever exercising `_with_index_lock_retry`
(`gitutil.py:110-129`). VALIDATED.

## What
1. Add an instrumented test seam to `_with_index_lock_retry` in `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py`,
   using the codebase's standard test-seam pattern (a module-level callable defaulting to
   `None`, set from tests via pytest `monkeypatch.setattr` which auto-restores it): a
   module-level `_retry_probe: Callable[[int, subprocess.CompletedProcess], None] | None = None`.
   - **Type of `result`:** the `subprocess.CompletedProcess` returned by `run_once()` — the
     SAME type `run_once` already returns (`Callable[[], subprocess.CompletedProcess]`). Tests
     inspect `result.returncode` and reuse the module's own `_is_index_lock_error(result.stderr
     or result.stdout or "")` to assert the first attempt was a genuine index.lock error.
   - **Where it fires (every attempt, incl. the pre-loop call):** invoked once immediately
     after the INITIAL `run_once()` at `gitutil.py:120` (as `attempt_number=1`), and again
     immediately after each in-loop `run_once()` at `gitutil.py:128`. `attempt_number` for the
     in-loop calls is `attempt + 1` where the loop variable `attempt` runs `1..
     _INDEX_LOCK_ATTEMPTS-1` — so the sequence observed is 1, 2, 3, … matching real attempts.
   - **Production is unaffected:** the default is `None`, so the invocation is guarded
     (`if _retry_probe is not None: _retry_probe(...)`) and skipped in production. Callers that
     import `_with_index_lock_retry` by name (e.g. `event_append.py:45,211,219`) see no behavior
     change because the seam stays `None` outside tests. The probe is invoked directly (not
     wrapped in try/except): in production it is never set, and a test owns its own probe's
     behavior, so a probe raising surfaces as that test's own failure — the desired contract.
   - **No teardown footgun:** tests set the seam via pytest `monkeypatch.setattr(gitutil,
     "_retry_probe", …)`, which auto-restores it to `None` at test teardown (no manual reset,
     no cross-test leakage) — the standard way module-level callables are patched in this suite.
2. Rewrite the three contended-lock tests to use the seam instead of `threading.Timer`: plant a
   fresh (young, not reclaimable-as-stale) lock; the probe, on observing the first attempt fail
   with an index.lock error, unlinks the planted lock so the NEXT attempt succeeds (deterministic
   synchronization — the lock is released strictly after the confirmed first failure, never
   before). Assert at least two attempts occurred and that attempt 1's result was a genuine
   index.lock error. The now-unused `threading.Timer`-based `_plant_fresh_lock` timer branch is
   removed/updated so no test still relies on timing.
3. Add a dedicated non-lock-failure test in each of the two files: using the same seam to count
   attempts, drive a NON-index.lock git failure (e.g. a commit that fails for an unrelated
   reason) and assert the probe saw exactly ONE attempt (the non-lock failure returns
   immediately, never retried).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` — add the `_retry_probe` seam to `_with_index_lock_retry`
  (guarded, default None; fires on the pre-loop attempt and every in-loop attempt).
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_compact_index_lock.py` — deterministic ride-out test +
  a non-lock-not-retried test; drop the `threading.Timer` reliance.
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_delete_init_index_lock.py` — deterministic ride-out tests
  (delete + init) + a non-lock-not-retried test; update/remove the timer-based
  `_plant_fresh_lock` branch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] First attempt deterministically returns the real `index.lock` error before the lock is
      released (synchronized via the `_retry_probe` seam, not a timer).
- [ ] Each rewritten contended-lock test asserts at least two attempts occurred (retry path
      exercised) and that the first attempt was a genuine index.lock error.
- [ ] A dedicated test in each file asserts a non-lock git failure returns on the FIRST
      attempt and is never retried (probe observed exactly one attempt).
- [ ] The final event/commit (snapshot/tombstone) exists; `make test` green.
compiled_state.title: Contended index.lock ride-out tests must assert first-failure + retry count
